title: Level 0.02
wikitext:
{{Rewrite in Progress|progress=[[User:AzadDasher/Level 801|Take a look]]|date=June 30, 2026|author=Gravista|rewriter=AzadDasher|reason=This is a great start! I would recommend to try to make the most use out of the kenophobia aspect (e.g. you can do an addendum, although don’t make the addendum just repeat the same information the reader already knows about—that makes it filler), as well as write more about the auditory and visual hallucinations since they’re your only interesting thing going on in this sublevel. Some events occuring could be nice as well. Also elaborate how this is apparently the sister level of [[Level 0.01]]. Happy writing! ''Don’t forget to add an author & credits section at the bottom of the page as well.'' -Savoury}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 0.02: "Kenophobia"}}
{{Quote|When will this end?!|Mark Robertson, a survivor of Level 0.02.|color=#ffffff}}{{Alt Class|no=0.02|noname=2|ext=1|env=3|ent=0}}
'''Description'''
'''Level 0.02''', more commonly known as "Kenophobia" is believed to be the sister level of ''[[Level 0.01|Level 0.01.]]'' . The Level appears very similar to Level 0, resembling an office space, however the largest difference between Level 0.02 and its parent level is the lack of walls and the fact it appears to stretch an infinite distance. The flooring of this level consists of an old, moist carpet, appearing identical to that which covers the floor of its parent level. Above, The ceiling consists of various endless rows of ceiling tiles, each spanning an approximate distance of 2ft in each direction. Every tenth tile is reportedly replaced by a fluorescent light in a grid-like pattern. Due to the lack of walls, the reason the ceiling does not collapse in on itself is unclear, although it is theorised to be an anomalous property of this sub-level. Since the Sub-Level lacks walls, the humming originating from the fluorescent lights is amplified greatly. This can result in an occasional deafening droning noise which is documented to potentially cause damage to one's hearing. Despite possessing a seemingly infinite size, the air within this level is documented to be full of dust particles, limiting wanderers' vision, commonly creating a white or yellow haze in the distance. Due to this dust, wanderers who enter this level are commonly advised to leave consistent landmarks if possible as a way to determine where they have travelled this far.{{Blurimage ALT|align=center|file=Level_0.02_p.png|hovertext=dd|border-color=yellow|caption=The first picture of Level 0.02, taken in May 1981.}}Level 0.02 is documented to become increasingly unstable the further out you venture, with Wanderers encountering a phenomena nicknamed "Structural Decay". Hallucinations in this level are common, ranging from minor auditory hallucinations to visual hallucinations. Many wanderers have reported seeing dark figures in the distance and running in another direction, however when looking back, the figure had seemingly vanished. It is crucial to bring ''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]'' and other resources to this level to avoid going insane. There are also additional reports of wanderers hearing knocks or feeling a warm breath on the back of their neck. Due to the absence of documented entities, and the lack of reported harm, these are most likely to be hallucinations. Communication inside of this level is not possible due to the absence of internet-dependent infrastructure. GPS will not function, and a compass will spin rapidly in random directions, rendering it useless. Wanderers cannot encounter one another due to the same [[Phenomenon 15|isolation]] effect present in it's [[Level 0|parent level]]. Its also theorised that '''Level 0.02''' is a vast vein system that connects to [[Level 0.01]], Level 0 and [[The Palace in the Void|The Void]].
'''Anomalous Proportions'''
'''PHENOMENON 23'''
"Structural Decay"
{{Phenomenon Class}} '''Level 0.02''' is subject to a variety of atypical properties, labelled "Anomalous Properties". The most documented and commonly known anomaly Level 0.02 is host to is known as "Structural Decay", which results in most wanderers exiting the level via The Void. Structural Decay is a well-documented phenomena in '''Level 0.02''', becoming quickly amplified the further out wanderers venture into the level. Upon first appearing in Level 0.02, it will appear as described. After voyaging out an approximate distance of 5 kilometres, one may encounter small cracks in the ceiling or carpet, revealing a pitch-black location surrounding the level. One may also begin to encounter fallen ceiling tiles after journeying a distance of more than 5 kilometres, which slowly become increasingly common. These fallen tiles reveal a white crawlspace above the tiles which one may access on occasion. After walking for 7 kilometers, blackouts occur while the lights are reported to occasionally flicker, with more ceiling tiles scattered on the ground. After walking for 10 kilometers, some lights are damaged or completely broken, revealing exposed wires. After walking for 15 kilometers, most lights are broken except for only a select few, and many ceiling tiles cover the floor. Significant portions of carpet are ripped open, revealing wet, moldy and rotting wooden flooring beneath. The floor has sharp edges that may injure wanderers, making it crucial to stay alert. After walking for 20 kilometers, the level begins to warp, twist, and turn, presenting a major environmental threat to wanderers. After walking for 50 kilometers, the level completely breaks apart and appears to be crumbling away. Wanderers are advised to avoid proceeding past this point to prevent ending up in ''[[The Void]].'' Depending on the level you are, it can also change the form the structural decay destroys.
'''Entrances and Exits'''
=== '''Entrances''' === * Encountering a white doorframe in ''[[Level 0.01]]'' or ''[[Level 0]]'' and entering it will transport one here. ==== '''Exits''' ==== * Entering the small crawl space above the ceiling tiles will lead to ''[[Level 0]]''. * Finding a white door with a sign saying "0.23" will lead to ''[[Level 0.23]]''. * Journeying 50 kilometers in any direction will result in one's transportation to ''[[The Void]]'', a feat which only 10 have accomplished. * Discovering a staircase leading down to a very long hallway takes one to ''[[Level 0.01]]''. * Finding a large pool of water on the floor and entering will cause one to blackout and awaken in ''[[Level 37]]''.
